message:
fix(btrfs): reject trailing root_ref payload bytes (bd-35ys1)

parse_root_ref previously accepted arbitrary trailing bytes after the
declared name_len. The packed btrfs_root_ref item is exactly the
18-byte header followed by name_len bytes — anything past that is
malformed and should not silently parse. Now returns
ParseError::InvalidField {
    field: "root_ref.name_len",
    reason: "does not match payload length",
}
when data.len() > 18 + name_len, mirroring the rejection behavior we
already use for short payloads.

Test changes:

* crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs unit test root_ref_minimal_payload
  formerly added 16 trailing bytes and asserted they were ignored;
  flipped to assert the new rejection error so the contract is pinned
  in both directions (short AND long).
* crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs gains
  enumerate_subvolumes_trailing_root_ref_uses_fallback_name to prove
  enumeration tolerates a malformed ROOT_REF item by falling back to
  the synthetic "subvol-<id>" name rather than failing the whole
  walk — matters because real btrfs images can carry stale items
  alongside the live tree.
* crates/ffs-btrfs/tests/conformance_ondisk_golden.rs gains
  root_ref_trailing_payload_rejected as the conformance-side mirror of
  the unit test, so the golden harness catches any regression that
  re-introduces the lenient behavior.

Doc updates:

* FEATURE_PARITY.md — added "parse_root_ref() rejects ROOT_REF
  payloads with trailing bytes after the declared name" to the btrfs
  item-payload-decode row.
* conformance/COVERAGE.md — new BtrfsRootRef row (3/3 = 100%) and
  bumped the btrfs MUST clause total from 72 to 75.

Beads: bd-35ys1 (in_progress, assigned cod-fs).

 {"id":"bd-32yn.7","title":"E2E test: xfstests generic and ext4 subsets","description":"# E2E test: xfstests generic and ext4 subsets

## GOAL
Run the standard Linux filesystem test suite (xfstests/fstests) against FrankenFS FUSE mount to verify compatibility with expected filesystem semantics.

## BACKGROUND
xfstests (github.com/kdave/xfstests) is the authoritative filesystem test suite used by all major Linux filesystems. It contains:
- generic/: tests applicable to all POSIX filesystems (~600 tests)
- ext4/: tests specific to ext4 semantics (~100 tests)

## APPROACH

### Phase 1: Generic Subset
Run xfstests generic/ tests that apply to FUSE filesystems. Exclude tests requiring:
- Direct I/O (O_DIRECT) — FUSE may not support
- Filesystem-specific ioctls
- Quota management
- ACLs (unless we implement them)
- Kernel-specific features (fiemap, etc.)

Expected pass rate: start with identifying which tests pass, create tracking for regressions.

### Phase 2: ext4 Subset
Run xfstests ext4/ tests. Many require mkfs.ext4 features we may not support yet.
Subset: tests covering basic file operations, directory operations, extent handling.

### Infrastructure
- xfstests requires: TEST_DEV (FrankenFS FUSE mount), SCRATCH_DEV (second mount for temp data)
- Wrapper script: configure xfstests for FrankenFS, run selected tests, capture results
- CI integration: run on every PR, track pass/fail count over time

### Known Exclusions
- Tests requiring root (CAP_SYS_ADMIN)
- Tests requiring kernel features not available in FUSE
- Tests for features we explicitly don't implement (quotas, ACLs, etc.)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] xfstests infrastructure configured for FrankenFS
- [ ] Generic test subset identified and documented
- [ ] ext4 test subset identified and documented
- [ ] CI integration: test results tracked per commit
- [ ] Pass rate monotonically increases over time
- [ ] Regression: any previously passing test that fails blocks merge","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:22.445755731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T21:31:27.906718473Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T21:31:27.906686864Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","e2e","xfstests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.7","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:22.445755731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":124,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Added scripts/e2e/ffs_xfstests_e2e.sh (auto/plan/run modes with strict skip/fail control), curated subset manifests scripts/e2e/xfstests_generic.list + scripts/e2e/xfstests_ext4.list, README docs updates, CI planning gate step in .github/workflows/ci.yml, and FEATURE_PARITY note (partial status). Validation: bash -n scripts/e2e/ffs_xfstests_e2e.sh; XFSTESTS_MODE=plan XFSTESTS_STRICT=1 ./scripts/e2e/ffs_xfstests_e2e.sh (pass); XFSTESTS_MODE=run XFSTESTS_STRICT=0 ./scripts/e2e/ffs_xfstests_e2e.sh (expected skip without xfstests checkout); XFSTESTS_MODE=run XFSTESTS_DIR=/data/tmp/tmp.87MidCWD0V/xfstests XFSTESTS_DRY_RUN=1 XFSTESTS_STRICT=0 ./scripts/e2e/ffs_xfstests_e2e.sh (expected skip due missing xfstests build prereqs: fsstress not found).","created_at":"2026-02-16T21:07:02Z"},{"id":225,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.7","author":"SnowyMeadow","text":"Progress 2026-02-16 (phase 2): upgraded xfstests E2E to emit structured per-commit artifacts and support regression gating. Changes: (1) scripts/e2e/ffs_xfstests_e2e.sh now writes selected_tests.txt + summary.json + results.json + junit.xml in plan/run/skip paths; (2) added scripts/e2e/xfstests_regression_guard.json with must_pass/min_pass_count/min_pass_rate policy; (3) run mode parses check output and enforces guard when not in dry-run; (4) CI failure artifact upload now includes xfstests XML; (5) README + FEATURE_PARITY updated to document tracking + guard behavior. Validation: bash -n script, plan mode pass, run mode skip with and without explicit XFSTESTS_DIR when prereqs missing, strict mode correctly fails. Workspace gates via rch: cargo fmt --check (pass), cargo check --workspace --all-targets (remote exit=0), cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (remote exit=0), cargo test --workspace (remote exit=0). Note: rch intermittently stalls after remote success during artifact retrieval in some sessions; remote exit codes were 0 in logs.","created_at":"2026-02-16T21:31:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-32yn.8","title":"Add unit tests for fault injection framework harness","description":"# Add unit tests for fault injection framework harness

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the Testing and Fault Injection epic (bd-32yn). The epic has many E2E/integration test beads (bd-32yn.1 through bd-32yn.7) but NO unit tests for the fault injection framework itself -- the pluggable I/O layer, failure point registration, deterministic replay, and crash-point scheduling.

## TEST PLAN

### Pluggable I/O Layer Tests
1. FaultInjector wraps BlockDevice transparently (no faults = passthrough)
2. fail_at_byte(offset) triggers fault at exact byte
3. fail_on_read(block) returns injected error
4. fail_on_write(block) returns injected error
5. Fault cleared after trigger (one-shot mode)
6. Persistent fault mode (fault on every access)

### Deterministic Replay Tests
7. Same seed produces same fault sequence
8. Fault sequence is reproducible across runs
9. Fault log captures all injected faults with timestamps

### Crash Point Tests
10. Crash point registered at specific operation
11. Crash point fires at correct moment (before/after fsync)
12. Recovery after crash point: filesystem mounts cleanly

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 12 tests above. Deterministic.

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Fault injected (debug): fault_type, target_block, operation
- Crash point fired (info): crash_point_id, operation_in_flight

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 12 tests pass
2. Framework is reusable across all fault injection beads
3. Deterministic replay verified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IronPeak","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:32.985112387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T22:09:55.902732223Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T22:09:55.902631273Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["faultinjection","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.8","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:32.985112387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":156,"issue_id":"bd-32yn.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Reject trailing bytes, update focused parser/enumeration coverage, refresh parity docs, and validate with targeted ffs-btrfs gates.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T16:47:36.268063376Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T16:48:28.325589743Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-361j","title":"verify_ext4_integrity reports false-positive inode checksum failures for unallocated inodes","description":"verify_ext4_integrity() checks inode checksums for ALL inodes that read_inode succeeds on, including unallocated inodes with stale data. On a clean mkfs'd ext4_small.img (16MB), it reports 55 failures and 18 passes, marking the image as unhealthy. The function should consult the inode bitmap to skip unallocated inodes. This is a correctness bug in the integrity CLI subcommand.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-25T11:40:31.001120962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T11:51:29.238425507Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T11:51:29.238353412Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-375","title":"EPIC: btrfs Read Path & FUSE Mount","description":"# EPIC: btrfs Read Path & FUSE Mount

## PURPOSE
Enable read-only btrfs FUSE mounting, achieving parity with ext4 read-only support. Currently btrfs metadata parsing is at 35% (7/20).

## BACKGROUND
From FEATURE_PARITY.md, btrfs gaps:
- btrfs transaction parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- btrfs delayed refs parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- btrfs scrub parity: ❌ NOT IMPLEMENTED
- FUSE mount for btrfs: NOT WORKING

However, significant foundation exists:
- Superblock decode: ✅
- B-tree header decode: ✅
- Leaf item metadata decode: ✅
- Internal node parsing: ✅
- sys_chunk mapping: ✅
- Read-only tree walk: ✅
- BtrfsContext in ffs-core: ✅

The remaining gap is wiring tree walking and extent resolution into `FsOps` and then into the FUSE surface.

## CURRENT STATE
- `ffs-ondisk` has comprehensive btrfs parsing
- `ffs-btrfs` provides `walk_tree`
- `ffs-core` has a `BtrfsContext` stub
- `ffs-fuse` operations currently route to ext4-only `FsOps`

## GAPS TO CLOSE
1. Implement `BtrfsFsOps` (like `Ext4FsOps` but for btrfs)
2. Wire `ffs-cli mount` path to btrfs ops
3. Implement btrfs inode -> extent resolution sufficient for read-only file reads
4. Add E2E coverage for btrfs mount

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount btrfs.img /mnt` works read-only
2. Basic file operations work: `ls`, `cat`, `stat`
3. Subvolume visibility (at least default subvol)
4. Performance baseline established (hyperfine) for inspect + mount smoke
5. btrfs metadata parsing reaches 60%+ parity

## DEPENDENCIES
- EPIC: Conformance & Quality Infrastructure (bd-2jk) - need fixtures/goldens
- btrfs golden fixtures (bd-2jk.2)

## EXPLICIT V1 EXCLUSIONS (per PLAN_TO_PORT_FRANKENFS_TO_RUST.md)
- Multi-device / RAID: OUT OF SCOPE
- Compression: OUT OF SCOPE
- Send/receive: OUT OF SCOPE
- Write support: OUT OF SCOPE

## RELATED SPEC SECTIONS
- EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md §4-6 (btrfs behavior)
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md §2 (btrfs format)

## Success Criteria
1. `ffs inspect <btrfs.img>` and `ffs mount <btrfs.img>` work on the golden fixtures without panics.
2. `scripts/e2e/ffs_btrfs_ro_smoke.sh` passes (or skips cleanly when FUSE unavailable) and produces actionable logs.
3. Unit tests cover:
   - inode lookup by key
   - directory enumeration ordering/offsets
   - extent resolution for file reads","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:56:58.365480057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:32.043983776Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:32.043968297Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","core","fuse"],"comments":[{"id":144,"issue_id":"bd-375","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: runtime_backpressure
Mapped graveyard sections: 0.5 Graceful Degradation + 10.3 Learning-Augmented Online + 12.13 Change-point
EV score: 16 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: queue_budget=1024, throttle_sleep_ms<=25, shed_ratio_cap=0.15
Fallback trigger: deterministic threshold gate with Emergency=read-only

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:22Z"}]}

@@ -48,7 +48,7 @@
 | btrfs internal node parsing | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | `parse_internal_items` in `ffs-ondisk` |
 | btrfs sys_chunk mapping | `fs/btrfs/volumes.c` | ✅ | `map_logical_to_physical` in `ffs-ondisk`, with proptest + metamorphic coverage in `crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs` for translation covariance, chunk-order permutation invariance, and Single-vs-DUP primary mapping equivalence. |
 | btrfs read-only tree walk | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | `walk_tree` in `ffs-btrfs` |
-| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. `parse_root_item()` reads level/UUID fields from the packed kernel layout, invalidates UUID-era fields when `generation_v2` is stale, and rejects impossible tree levels; `parse_inode_item()` rejects impossible timestamp nanoseconds before they reach `Duration`/`SystemTime` conversion paths; and `parse_extent_data()` rejects unsupported extent compression values and nonzero encryption/other-encoding fields instead of treating encoded payloads as raw readable data. |
+| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. `parse_root_item()` reads level/UUID fields from the packed kernel layout, invalidates UUID-era fields when `generation_v2` is stale, and rejects impossible tree levels; `parse_root_ref()` rejects ROOT_REF payloads with trailing bytes after the declared name; `parse_inode_item()` rejects impossible timestamp nanoseconds before they reach `Duration`/`SystemTime` conversion paths; and `parse_extent_data()` rejects unsupported extent compression values and nonzero encryption/other-encoding fields instead of treating encoded payloads as raw readable data. |
 | btrfs open/validate pipeline | `fs/btrfs/disk-io.c` | ✅ | `BtrfsContext` in `ffs-core` |
 | btrfs transaction parity | `fs/btrfs/transaction.c` | ✅ | Transaction begin/commit/abort semantics and integration paths are implemented with coverage in unit + E2E suites. |
 | btrfs delayed refs parity | `fs/btrfs/delayed-ref.c` | ✅ | `DelayedRefQueue` + `BtrfsRef` model + bounded flush/refcount tracking in `ffs-btrfs::BtrfsExtentAllocator`, with queue/refcount/stress tests. The allocator now also exposes fragmentation-aware largest-free-extent discovery from block-group/extent-tree gaps, and `OpenFs::largest_contiguous_free_run` uses that value for btrfs instead of reporting total free bytes as a conservative upper bound. |
